content.question: Whether the Finnish cost-saving rationale for closures (per-pupil economy of scale) is empirically realized at the municipal level in Hungary, or whether transport, special-education provisioning, and opportunity costs (loss of community services bundled with schools) offset administrative savings.
content.reduction_path: Request freedom-of-information disclosure of municipal-level cost accounting before and after school closure (or consolidation) in a sample of 10–15 Hungarian village/small-town local governments, specifying categorization of transport, central administration, teacher-redeployment, and building-maintenance costs over 5-year windows.
